children.heading: Short title
children.label: a Short title
children.text: This section may be cited as the “Survivors of Human Trafficking Empowerment Act”.

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: b Establishment
children.text: There is established the United States Advisory Council on Human Trafficking (referred to in this section as the “Council”), which shall provide advice and recommendations to the Senior Policy Operating Group established under (referred to in this section as the “Group”) and the President’s Interagency Task Force to Monitor and Combat Trafficking established under section 7103(a) of such title (referred to in this section as the “Task Force”).section 7103(g) of this title

children.heading: Composition
children.label: 1 Composition
children.text: The Council shall be composed of not less than 8 and not more than 14 individuals who are survivors of human trafficking.

children.label: A
children.text: survivors of sex trafficking and survivors of labor trafficking; and

children.label: B
children.text: survivors who are United States citizens and survivors who are aliens lawfully present in the United States.

children.heading: Appointment
children.label: 3 Appointment
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , the President shall appoint the members of the Council.May 29, 2015

children.heading: Term; reappointment
children.label: 4 Term; reappointment
children.text: Each member of the Council shall serve for a term of 2 years and may be reappointed by the President to serve 1 additional 2-year term.
be a nongovernmental advisory body to the Group;

meet, at its own discretion or at the request of the Group, not less frequently than annually to review Federal Government policy and programs intended to combat human trafficking, including programs relating to the provision of services for victims and serve as a point of contact for Federal agencies reaching out to human trafficking survivors for input on programming and policies relating to human trafficking in the United States;

formulate assessments and recommendations to ensure that policy and programming efforts of the Federal Government conform, to the extent practicable, to the best practices in the field of human trafficking prevention; and
meet with the Group not less frequently than annually, and not later than 45 days before a meeting with the Task Force, to formally present the findings and recommendations of the Council.
the chair of the Task Force;
the Committees on Foreign Affairs, Homeland Security, Appropriations, and the Judiciary of the House of Representatives; and
the Committees on Foreign Relations, Appropriations, Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs, and the Judiciary of the Senate.
shall not be considered employees of the Federal Government for any purpose;
shall receive travel expenses, including per diem in lieu of subsistence, in accordance with the applicable provisions under subchapter I of chapter 57 of title 5; and
may each receive compensation for each day such member is engaged in the actual performance of the duties of the Council.

children.heading: Nonapplicability of FACA
children.label: g Nonapplicability of FACA
children.text: The Council shall not be subject to the requirements under the Federal Advisory Committee Act (5 U.S.C. App.).1
